claim: Level 5 agents should proactively escalate to humans via notifications (Slack, email) when encountering issues only humans can resolve
actionable: Design agent workflows to detect unsolvable problems and escalate asynchronously via email/Slack rather than blocking or failing
source_title: Making AI Work: How to Build and Scale Long Running Enterprise Agents

claim: Agents should call humans when needed rather than requiring continuous human monitoring; this reduces operational burden
actionable: Implement agent escalation via async pull notifications so operators engage only when an issue occurs, not continuously
source_title: Making AI Work: How to Build and Scale Long Running Enterprise Agents

claim: Use Helm charts for repeatable, Kubernetes-native deployment of Agentex agents across environments
actionable: Deploy agents via published Helm charts to standardize rollout and enable version control of agent infrastructure
source_title: Making AI Work: How to Build and Scale Long Running Enterprise Agents

claim: agentics agents deploy CLI provides parameterizable deployment with overridable defaults for agent configuration
actionable: Use CLI commands to deploy agents with environment-specific overrides without manual configuration drift
source_title: Making AI Work: How to Build and Scale Long Running Enterprise Agents

claim: Long-running agents require persistent state management to survive crashes and resume without losing context
actionable: Implement checkpoints and state recovery mechanisms so agents resume work across failures without data loss
source_title: Making AI Work: How to Build and Scale Long Running Enterprise Agents
